speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Who keeps the gate here, ho?

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Where is the Earl?

speaker: PORTER
text: What shall I say you are?

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Tell thou the Earl

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: That the Lord Bardolph doth attend him here.

speaker: PORTER
text: His Lordship is walked forth into the orchard.

speaker: PORTER
text: Please it your Honor knock but at the gate

speaker: PORTER
text: And he himself will answer.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Here comes the Earl.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: What news, Lord Bardolph? Every minute now

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Should be the father of some stratagem.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The times are wild. Contention, like a horse

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Full of high feeding, madly hath broke loose

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And bears down all before him.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Noble earl,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: I bring you certain news from Shrewsbury.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Good, an God will!

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: As good as heart can wish.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: The King is almost wounded to the death,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And, in the fortune of my lord your son,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Prince Harry slain outright; and both the Blunts

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Killed by the hand of Douglas; young Prince John

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And Westmoreland and Stafford fled the field;

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And Harry Monmouth’s brawn, the hulk Sir John,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Is prisoner to your son. O, such a day,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: So fought, so followed, and so fairly won,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Came not till now to dignify the times

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Since Caesar’s fortunes.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: How is this derived?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Saw you the field? Came you from Shrewsbury?

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: I spake with one, my lord, that came from thence,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: A gentleman well bred and of good name,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: That freely rendered me these news for true.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Here comes my servant Travers, who I sent

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: On Tuesday last to listen after news.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: My lord, I overrode him on the way,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And he is furnished with no certainties

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: More than he haply may retail from me.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Now, Travers, what good tidings comes with you?

speaker: TRAVERS
text: My lord, Sir John Umfrevile turned me back

speaker: TRAVERS
text: With joyful tidings and, being better horsed,

speaker: TRAVERS
text: Outrode me. After him came spurring hard

speaker: TRAVERS
text: A gentleman, almost forspent with speed,

speaker: TRAVERS
text: That stopped by me to breathe his bloodied horse.

speaker: TRAVERS
text: He asked the way to Chester, and of him

speaker: TRAVERS
text: I did demand what news from Shrewsbury.

speaker: TRAVERS
text: He told me that rebellion had bad luck

speaker: TRAVERS
text: And that young Harry Percy’s spur was cold.

speaker: TRAVERS
text: With that he gave his able horse the head

speaker: TRAVERS
text: And, bending forward, struck his armèd heels

speaker: TRAVERS
text: Against the panting sides of his poor jade

speaker: TRAVERS
text: Up to the rowel-head, and starting so

speaker: TRAVERS
text: He seemed in running to devour the way,

speaker: TRAVERS
text: Staying no longer question.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Ha? Again:

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Said he young Harry Percy’s spur was cold?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Of Hotspur, Coldspur? That rebellion

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Had met ill luck?

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: My lord, I’ll tell you what:

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: If my young lord your son have not the day,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Upon mine honor, for a silken point

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: I’ll give my barony. Never talk of it.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Why should that gentleman that rode by Travers

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Give then such instances of loss?

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Who, he?

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: He was some hilding fellow that had stol’n

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: The horse he rode on and, upon my life,

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Spoke at a venture.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Look, here comes more news.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Yea, this man’s brow, like to a title leaf,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Foretells the nature of a tragic volume.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: So looks the strand whereon the imperious flood

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Hath left a witnessed usurpation.—

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Say, Morton, didst thou come from Shrewsbury?

speaker: MORTON
text: I ran from Shrewsbury, my noble lord,

speaker: MORTON
text: Where hateful death put on his ugliest mask

speaker: MORTON
text: To fright our party.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: How doth my son and brother?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Thou tremblest, and the whiteness in thy cheek

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Is apter than thy tongue to tell thy errand.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Even such a man, so faint, so spiritless,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: So dull, so dead in look, so woebegone,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Drew Priam’s curtain in the dead of night

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And would have told him half his Troy was burnt;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: But Priam found the fire ere he his tongue,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And I my Percy’s death ere thou report’st it.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: This thou wouldst say: “Your son did thus and thus;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Your brother thus; so fought the noble Douglas”—

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Stopping my greedy ear with their bold deeds.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: But in the end, to stop my ear indeed,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Thou hast a sigh to blow away this praise,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Ending with “Brother, son, and all are dead.”

speaker: MORTON
text: Douglas is living, and your brother yet,

speaker: MORTON
text: But for my lord your son—

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Why, he is dead.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: See what a ready tongue suspicion hath!

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: He that but fears the thing he would not know

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Hath, by instinct, knowledge from others’ eyes

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That what he feared is chancèd. Yet speak, Morton.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Tell thou an earl his divination lies,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And I will take it as a sweet disgrace

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And make thee rich for doing me such wrong.

speaker: MORTON
text: You are too great to be by me gainsaid,

speaker: MORTON
text: Your spirit is too true, your fears too certain.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Yet, for all this, say not that Percy’s dead.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: I see a strange confession in thine eye.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Thou shak’st thy head and hold’st it fear or sin

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: To speak a truth. If he be slain, say so.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The tongue offends not that reports his death;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And he doth sin that doth belie the dead,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Not he which says the dead is not alive.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Yet the first bringer of unwelcome news

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Hath but a losing office, and his tongue

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Sounds ever after as a sullen bell

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Remembered tolling a departing friend.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: I cannot think, my lord, your son is dead.

speaker: MORTON
text: I am sorry I should force you to believe

speaker: MORTON
text: That which I would to God I had not seen,

speaker: MORTON
text: But these mine eyes saw him in bloody state,

speaker: MORTON
text: Rend’ring faint quittance, wearied and outbreathed,

speaker: MORTON
text: To Harry Monmouth, whose swift wrath beat down

speaker: MORTON
text: The never-daunted Percy to the earth,

speaker: MORTON
text: From whence with life he never more sprung up.

speaker: MORTON
text: In few, his death, whose spirit lent a fire

speaker: MORTON
text: Even to the dullest peasant in his camp,

speaker: MORTON
text: Being bruited once, took fire and heat away

speaker: MORTON
text: From the best-tempered courage in his troops;

speaker: MORTON
text: For from his mettle was his party steeled,

speaker: MORTON
text: Which, once in him abated, all the rest

speaker: MORTON
text: Turned on themselves, like dull and heavy lead.

speaker: MORTON
text: And as the thing that’s heavy in itself

speaker: MORTON
text: Upon enforcement flies with greatest speed,

speaker: MORTON
text: So did our men, heavy in Hotspur’s loss,

speaker: MORTON
text: Lend to this weight such lightness with their fear

speaker: MORTON
text: That arrows fled not swifter toward their aim

speaker: MORTON
text: Than did our soldiers, aiming at their safety,

speaker: MORTON
text: Fly from the field. Then was that noble Worcester

speaker: MORTON
text: So soon ta’en prisoner; and that furious Scot,

speaker: MORTON
text: The bloody Douglas, whose well-laboring sword

speaker: MORTON
text: Had three times slain th’ appearance of the King,

speaker: MORTON
text: Gan vail his stomach and did grace the shame

speaker: MORTON
text: Of those that turned their backs and in his flight,

speaker: MORTON
text: Stumbling in fear, was took. The sum of all

speaker: MORTON
text: Is that the King hath won and hath sent out

speaker: MORTON
text: A speedy power to encounter you, my lord,

speaker: MORTON
text: Under the conduct of young Lancaster

speaker: MORTON
text: And Westmoreland. This is the news at full.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: For this I shall have time enough to mourn.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: In poison there is physic, and these news,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Having been well, that would have made me sick,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Being sick, have in some measure made me well.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And as the wretch whose fever-weakened joints,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Like strengthless hinges, buckle under life,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Impatient of his fit, breaks like a fire

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Out of his keeper’s arms, even so my limbs,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Weakened with grief, being now enraged with grief,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Are thrice themselves. Hence therefore, thou nice crutch.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: A scaly gauntlet now with joints of steel

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Must glove this hand. And hence, thou sickly coif.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Thou art a guard too wanton for the head

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Which princes, fleshed with conquest, aim to hit.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Now bind my brows with iron, and approach

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The ragged’st hour that time and spite dare bring

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: To frown upon th’ enraged Northumberland.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Let heaven kiss Earth! Now let not Nature’s hand

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Keep the wild flood confined. Let order die,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And let this world no longer be a stage

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: To feed contention in a lingering act;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: But let one spirit of the firstborn Cain

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Reign in all bosoms, that, each heart being set

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: On bloody courses, the rude scene may end,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And darkness be the burier of the dead.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: This strainèd passion doth you wrong, my lord.

speaker: MORTON
text: Sweet earl, divorce not wisdom from your honor.

speaker: MORTON
text: The lives of all your loving complices

speaker: MORTON
text: Lean on your health, the which, if you give o’er

speaker: MORTON
text: To stormy passion, must perforce decay.

speaker: MORTON
text: You cast th’ event of war, my noble lord,

speaker: MORTON
text: And summed the accompt of chance before you said

speaker: MORTON
text: “Let us make head.” It was your presurmise

speaker: MORTON
text: That in the dole of blows your son might drop.

speaker: MORTON
text: You knew he walked o’er perils on an edge,

speaker: MORTON
text: More likely to fall in than to get o’er.

speaker: MORTON
text: You were advised his flesh was capable

speaker: MORTON
text: Of wounds and scars, and that his forward spirit

speaker: MORTON
text: Would lift him where most trade of danger ranged.

speaker: MORTON
text: Yet did you say “Go forth,” and none of this,

speaker: MORTON
text: Though strongly apprehended, could restrain

speaker: MORTON
text: The stiff-borne action. What hath then befall’n,

speaker: MORTON
text: Or what did this bold enterprise bring forth,

speaker: MORTON
text: More than that being which was like to be?

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: We all that are engagèd to this loss

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Knew that we ventured on such dangerous seas

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: That if we wrought out life, ’twas ten to one;

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And yet we ventured, for the gain proposed

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Choked the respect of likely peril feared;

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: And since we are o’erset, venture again.

speaker:
LORD
               
              BARDOLPH
text: Come, we will all put forth, body and goods.

speaker: MORTON
text: ’Tis more than time.—And, my most noble lord,

speaker: MORTON
text: I hear for certain, and dare speak the truth:

speaker: MORTON
text: The gentle Archbishop of York is up

speaker: MORTON
text: With well-appointed powers. He is a man

speaker: MORTON
text: Who with a double surety binds his followers.

speaker: MORTON
text: My lord your son had only but the corpse,

speaker: MORTON
text: But shadows and the shows of men, to fight;

speaker: MORTON
text: For that same word “rebellion” did divide

speaker: MORTON
text: The action of their bodies from their souls,

speaker: MORTON
text: And they did fight with queasiness, constrained,

speaker: MORTON
text: As men drink potions, that their weapons only

speaker: MORTON
text: Seemed on our side. But, for their spirits and souls,

speaker: MORTON
text: This word “rebellion,” it had froze them up

speaker: MORTON
text: As fish are in a pond. But now the Bishop

speaker: MORTON
text: Turns insurrection to religion.

speaker: MORTON
text: Supposed sincere and holy in his thoughts,

speaker: MORTON
text: He’s followed both with body and with mind,

speaker: MORTON
text: And doth enlarge his rising with the blood

speaker: MORTON
text: Of fair King Richard, scraped from Pomfret stones;

speaker: MORTON
text: Derives from heaven his quarrel and his cause;

speaker: MORTON
text: Tells them he doth bestride a bleeding land,

speaker: MORTON
text: Gasping for life under great Bolingbroke;

speaker: MORTON
text: And more and less do flock to follow him.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: I knew of this before, but, to speak truth,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: This present grief had wiped it from my mind.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Go in with me and counsel every man

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The aptest way for safety and revenge.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Get posts and letters, and make friends with speed.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Never so few, and never yet more need.
